decisions.title: Outcome follows system state
decisions.decision: Settlement authority derives strictly from deterministic protocol state, bilateral participant confirmation, provider-confirmed payment state, bounded temporal rules, and immutable transaction metadata.

decisions.title: Stripe owns payment truth
decisions.decision: Stripe is the authoritative payment state provider. Vouch mirrors provider state locally and reconciles async provider events through idempotent webhook processing.

decisions.title: Authorization-first deferred capture
decisions.decision: Vouch uses Stripe manual-capture PaymentIntents with deferred settlement capture after bilateral confirmation succeeds.

decisions.title: No discretionary settlement layer
decisions.decision: Vouch does not implement disputes, appeals, arbitration, evidence review, screenshots, mediation, manual awards, support-issued settlement, or confirmation rewrites.

decisions.title: No marketplace behavior
decisions.decision: Vouch is not a marketplace, provider directory, discovery platform, messaging platform, review platform, or scheduling marketplace.

decisions.title: Readiness is embedded gating
decisions.decision: Payment readiness, payout readiness, identity readiness, terms acceptance, and verification readiness are embedded gating systems within canonical flows, not standalone settings/setup products.

decisions.title: Server authority architecture
decisions.decision: Protected reads go through fetchers. Protected writes go through server actions and transactions. Route shells remain thin orchestration layers.

decisions.title: Immutable pricing snapshots
decisions.decision: Amount, fee configuration, currency, participants, confirmation window, and provider references freeze at Vouch creation and do not recalculate dynamically later.

decisions.title: Deterministic operational recovery only
decisions.decision: Operational retry and reconciliation tooling may exist only for deterministic technical recovery. No operational tooling may introduce discretionary settlement authority.
